event_date_text: Shutterstock Shutterstock Calendar of events Get the latest on UNESCO activities worldwide Home All events Today at UNESCO HQ Working meetings Statutory meetings Governing bodies Delegates meetings Cultural events at HQ International Days Jump to : Next international days Calls for applications and statutory deadlines This Month at UNESCO Launch of Disaster Risk Reduction (DRR) innovation hub 05 March 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris, France) Disaster risk reduction (DRR) and climate adaptation (CCA) efforts are often scattered, making it challenging for governments, communities, and practitioners to find the right tools and solutions when they’re needed most. The UNESCO Digital Innovation Hub for Disaster Risk Reduction changes this by connecting governments, scientists, innovators, and communities with trusted DRR and CCA tools, services, and expertise on one global, centralized platform - helping turn proven solutions into real-world action, across all phases of disaster management. International Women’s Day 08 March 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris, France) In 2026 UNESCO joins the UN family in marking this day under the theme, “Rights. Justice. Action. For ALL Women and Girls". For UNESCO, this theme speaks to the everyday realities of women and girls: their right to learn, to express themselves freely, to access information, to be safe in digital and public spaces, and to participate fully in cultural, scientific and civic life. Higher education today and tomorrow: Launch of UNESCO Roadmap 12 March 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris, France) This event will serve as a global platform for policymakers, higher education leaders, faculty, researchers, students, international organizations, civil society and private sector partners to advance knowledge, debate and action that will connect higher education today with the possibilities and opportunities of tomorrow. It will feature the launch of the following publications: Transforming higher education: Global collaboration on visioning and action (roadmap) The global observatory on higher education (by IESALC) G7 High-level event on education financing & High-level Steering Committee Sherpa Meeting on SDG4 13 March 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris, France) The official G7 French presidency high-level policy debate is jointly organized by UNESCO and France, under France’s G7 Presidency, in close collaboration with the Global Partnership for Education (GPE) and other partners. The high-level policy debate aims to advance a common policy narrative and political momentum around international partnerships through sustainable financing of education. World Day for Glaciers and World Water Day 18-19 March 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris, France) UNESCO will celebrate the World Day for Glaciers and the World Water Day at its Headquarters in Paris, closing the International Year of Glaciers' Preservation 2025 and launching the new Decade of Action for Cryospheric Sciences with dedicated sessions and side events highlighting the vital links between cryosphere, water and gender equality. 2026 Global Education Coalition sixth annual meeting 23-24 March 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris, France) Under the theme “Turning vision into value: Transforming education together,” the meeting will explore how investments in digital transformation and AI, when embedded within system-wide approaches, can generate positive social and economic outcomes for learners, teachers, and societies. The public event witll be on the 24th of March. High-level launch of the 2026 GEM Report Countdown to 2030: Access and equity 25 March 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris France) The high-level launch of the 2026 GEM Report will take place on 25 March, back-to-back with the Global Education Coalition's sixth annual meeting, taking place on March 23 and 24. The event will be divided into two parts: The morning session will feature opening remarks, a presentation of the 2026 GEM Report, a ministerial panel on access and equity and ministerial panel to debate policies to address the out-of-school population. The afternoon session will feature thematic policy discussions co-organized with UNESCO and partners on different aspects of the access and equity theme. conference_title: Higher education today and tomorrow: Launch of UNESCO Roadmap
event_date_text: Higher education today and tomorrow: Launch of UNESCO Roadmap 12 March 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris, France) This event will serve as a global platform for policymakers, higher education leaders, faculty, researchers, students, international organizations, civil society and private sector partners to advance knowledge, debate and action that will connect higher education today with the possibilities and opportunities of tomorrow. It will feature the launch of the following publications: Transforming higher education: Global collaboration on visioning and action (roadmap) The global observatory on higher education (by IESALC)

event_date_text: G7 High-level event on education financing & High-level Steering Committee Sherpa Meeting on SDG4 13 March 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris, France) The official G7 French presidency high-level policy debate is jointly organized by UNESCO and France, under France’s G7 Presidency, in close collaboration with the Global Partnership for Education (GPE) and other partners. The high-level policy debate aims to advance a common policy narrative and political momentum around international partnerships through sustainable financing of education.

conference_title: World Day for Glaciers and World Water Day
event_date_text: World Day for Glaciers and World Water Day 18-19 March 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris, France) UNESCO will celebrate the World Day for Glaciers and the World Water Day at its Headquarters in Paris, closing the International Year of Glaciers' Preservation 2025 and launching the new Decade of Action for Cryospheric Sciences with dedicated sessions and side events highlighting the vital links between cryosphere, water and gender equality.

conference_title: 2026 Global Education Coalition sixth annual meeting
event_date_text: 2026 Global Education Coalition sixth annual meeting 23-24 March 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris, France) Under the theme “Turning vision into value: Transforming education together,” the meeting will explore how investments in digital transformation and AI, when embedded within system-wide approaches, can generate positive social and economic outcomes for learners, teachers, and societies. The public event witll be on the 24th of March.

conference_title: High-level launch of the 2026 GEM Report Countdown to 2030: Access and equity
event_date_text: High-level launch of the 2026 GEM Report Countdown to 2030: Access and equity 25 March 2026 (UNESCO HQ- Paris France) The high-level launch of the 2026 GEM Report will take place on 25 March, back-to-back with the Global Education Coalition's sixth annual meeting, taking place on March 23 and 24. The event will be divided into two parts: The morning session will feature opening remarks, a presentation of the 2026 GEM Report, a ministerial panel on access and equity and ministerial panel to debate policies to address the out-of-school population. The afternoon session will feature thematic policy discussions co-organized with UNESCO and partners on different aspects of the access and equity theme.

event_date_text: ABOUT ASEAN The Association of Southeast Asian Nations, or ASEAN, was established on 8 August 1967 in Bangkok, Thailand, with the signing of the ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration) by the Founding Fathers of ASEAN: Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines, Singapore and Thailand. Brunei Darussalam joined ASEAN on 7 January 1984, followed by Viet Nam on 28 July 1995, Lao PDR and Myanmar on 23 July 1997, Cambodia on 30 April 1999, and Timor-Leste on 25 October 2025 making up what is today the eleven Member States of ASEAN. event_date_text: UNESCO UNESCO General Conference The General Conference is the highest governing body of UNESCO. Home President 43rd Session Documents Previous Sessions Secretariat The General Conference determines the policies and the main lines of work of the Organization Its duty is to set the programmes and the budget of UNESCO. It also elects the Members of the Executive Board and appoints, every four years, the Director-General. The working languages of the General Conference are Arabic, Chinese, English, French, Russian and Spanish. The General Conference consists of the representatives of UNESCO's Member States. It meets every two years, and is attended by Member States and Associate Members, together with observers for non-Member States, intergovernmental organizations and non-governmental organizations (NGOs). Each country has one vote, irrespective of its size or the extent of its contribution to the budget. 43rd Session of the General Conference 30 October - 13 November 2025 Read more 42nd session of the General Conference 7 - 22 November 2023 Read more Fifth extraordinary session of the General Conference 29 - 30 June 2023 Read more
